headline: USDe transfers unpausable and sUSDe unstake is on-chain (cap 90d, currently dynamic 1-7d), but redemption to USDC at par requires KYC whitelist; retail exit depends on secondary-market liquidity.

findings.text: Exit functions enumerated across three contracts. USDe (0x4c9EDD...68B3): transfer/transferFrom/burn/burnFrom — standard ERC20, no redemption surface on the token itself. sUSDe StakedUSDeV2 (0x9D39A5...3497): cooldownAssets(uint256), cooldownShares(uint256), unstake(address) which pulls from USDeSilo after cooldown; ERC4626 withdraw()/redeem() are gated off when cooldownDuration > 0. EthenaMinting V2 (0xe3490297...1b62d3): mint(Order,Route,Signature), mintWETH, redeem(Order,Signature) — these are the only paths converting USDe to underlying collateral at par.

Access modifiers diverge sharply by function class. USDe.transfer/burn: no role gate, no pause guard — anyone holding USDe can move or burn it permissionlessly. sUSDe.cooldownAssets/cooldownShares/unstake: callable by any holder; the contract has no Pausable modifier on these, but holders flagged with FULL_RESTRICTED_STAKER_ROLE are blocked from receiving sUSDe (blacklist on the share token, not on the underlying USDe). EthenaMinting.mint/redeem: restricted to addresses with MINTER_ROLE/REDEEMER_ROLE respectively, with benefactor/beneficiary whitelisting; this is the access-control choke point — retail wallets cannot call redeem() directly.

Pause / freeze actor classes: USDe token has no pause function at all (verified via Etherscan read tab — no paused() getter, no PAUSE_ROLE). sUSDe owner is 0x3B0AAF6E6FCd4A7CEEf8c92C32dfeA9E64DC1862 (DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE) which can set cooldownDuration up to MAX_COOLDOWN_DURATION = 90 days, redistribute locked balances of FULL_RESTRICTED holders, and grant/revoke BLACKLIST_MANAGER_ROLE — equivalent to an indefinite freeze on a per-address basis but NOT a global pause of unstake. EthenaMinting has a disableMintRedeem() administrative gate; this halts mint+redeem flow, but only affects KYC-whitelisted counterparties since retail had no access path anyway.

Emergency vs governance distinction: I could not pin a separate emergency-multisig actor with a hard time-cap for the sUSDe admin or EthenaMinting admin; the docs describe role-holders abstractly without naming an emergency-only short-term role with auto-expiry. The DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE on sUSDe (held by the owner address above) appears to be the unilateral controller for cooldownDuration and blacklist actions with no on-chain timelock visible at the contract level.

Queued-redemption mechanics: sUSDe holders enter a per-user UserCooldown struct (cooldownEnd, underlyingAmount); funds move to the USDeSilo during cooldown and are only released by unstake() after cooldownEnd. Current cooldownDuration in storage is non-zero; Ethena governance moved to a dynamic 1-7 day cooldown effective March 2025 (gov.ethenafoundation.com proposal). Hard cap at the contract level: 90 days (MAX_COOLDOWN_DURATION). No documented daily withdrawal cap; the queue itself is not pausable as a whole, only individual addresses can be FULL_RESTRICTED.

Forced-exit / escape-hatch: there is no permissionless emergency-exit that converts USDe to USDC at par bypassing KYC. The de facto escape hatch is the secondary market (Curve USDe pools, Uniswap, sUSDe ERC4626 unstake → USDe → swap). This means worst-case adversarial-admin scenario for non-KYC users degrades to market-price exit, not stuck capital, AS LONG AS USDe transfers remain unpausable (which they are at the token level — no pause function exists).

Frontend independence verified: cooldownAssets/cooldownShares/unstake on sUSDe are public functions callable directly from Etherscan's Write tab or any wallet/script — confirmed by ABI inspection on 0x9D39A5...3497. USDe transfers are ERC20-standard. Mint/redeem on EthenaMinting requires off-chain signed Order with KYC-whitelisted benefactor address, so the on-chain function is reachable but useless without a pre-existing whitelist relationship — frontend is not the gate, KYC is.

steelman.red: Par redemption to USDC requires KYC whitelist enforced at the EthenaMinting role layer; non-whitelisted retail holders have NO contract path to convert USDe to underlying collateral and are entirely dependent on third-party AMM liquidity that can dry up in a stress event (UST-style depeg), and the DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE on sUSDe is a single owner address with no visible timelock that can extend cooldown to 90 days and blacklist individual holders.
steelman.orange: USDe is a standard ERC20 with no pause/blacklist function at all so token transfers cannot be frozen, sUSDe unstake is on-chain and time-bounded with a hard 90-day cap (currently 1-7 days dynamic), and the worst-case exit for non-KYC users is secondary-market price impact rather than locked capital — but redemption at par is gated by off-chain KYC whitelisting which is a meaningful exit constraint.
steelman.green: The token contract itself is non-upgradable with no pause and no blacklist on transfers, cooldown is currently 1-7 days well under the 7-day green threshold, claims of finalized cooldowns (unstake after cooldownEnd) are not pause-gated by any role, and exit functions are directly callable on-chain without any frontend dependency.

rationale.verdict: Choosing orange because the green steel-man ignores the par-redemption KYC gate (the USDC conversion path is role-restricted at the EthenaMinting contract — verified MINTER_ROLE/REDEEMER_ROLE access control on 0xe3490297...1b62d3) which means non-whitelisted retail must rely on secondary-market liquidity, and the red steel-man overstates impact because USDe.transfer has no pause/blacklist (verified absence of paused() and blacklist functions on 0x4c9EDD...68B3) so holders can always reach a DEX, and sUSDe unstake claims are not pause-gated post-cooldown — the realistic exit is on-chain but at potentially-non-par price, which is the orange profile.

evidence.shows: USDe token contract: standard ERC20, Ownable2Step, mint() restricted to designated minter, no pause function, no blacklist on transfer, renounceOwnership disabled, not a proxy

sUSDe StakedUSDeV2 vault: ERC4626 with cooldownAssets/cooldownShares/unstake, owner 0x3B0AAF6E6FCd4A7CEEf8c92C32dfeA9E64DC1862 holds DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE, MAX_COOLDOWN_DURATION=90 days, blacklist roles FULL_RESTRICTED/SOFT_RESTRICTED, USDeSilo for cooled funds
EthenaMinting V2: mint(Order,Route,Signature) and redeem(Order,Signature) gated by MINTER_ROLE/REDEEMER_ROLE; benefactor/beneficiary whitelisting; disableMintRedeem() admin halt; no proxy pattern

DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE controls cooldownDuration up to 90 days, can call rescueTokens (excluding USDe) and redistributeLockedAmounts; BLACKLIST_MANAGER_ROLE assigns FULL/SOFT restricted staker roles; rewarder transfers USDe rewards
All addresses must be whitelisted by Ethena Protocol after KYC/AML to mint/redeem USDe directly; US users excluded from the application; redemption is permissioned at the contract role layer

Two issuer entities (Ethena GmbH for EEA, Ethena BVI for non-EEA from Jan 2025); redemption conditional on possession of USDe, no Terms violation, no regulator/court action restricting redemption — explicit legal off-ramp risk
Governance proposal (effective March 2025) introducing dynamic cooldown 1-7 days based on USDe reserve liquid stablecoin balance; replaces static 7-day cooldown

Code4rena finding documenting that when cooldownDuration > 0 the ERC4626 withdraw/redeem are disabled and only cooldownAssets/cooldownShares paths are usable — confirms architectural separation of request placement vs claim

unknowns: E3: actor class of the sUSDe DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE owner 0x3B0AAF6E6FCd4A7CEEf8c92C32dfeA9E64DC1862 — could not confirm whether this is an EOA, a 2-of-3 multisig, or a Safe with governance signers without an additional explorer probe of the address itself; affects whether the 90-day cooldown extension is unilateral or multisig-gated

E4: whether EthenaMinting has a separate emergency-scoped time-capped role distinct from the standard admin — the disableMintRedeem() function actor and any auto-expiry timer not enumerated in the surfaced ABI excerpt
E5: whether per-block or per-day cumulative withdrawal caps exist on sUSDe.unstake or in the USDeSilo — not visible in the documented function set
E6: whether any permissionless redemption tier exists for jurisdictions outside EEA/BVI coverage or for amounts below a KYC threshold — docs are silent, redemption appears to be uniformly KYC-gated

admin_addresses.role: sUSDe StakedUSDeV2 owner / DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE (sets cooldownDuration up to 90d, blacklist control)

protocol_metadata.about: Ethena issues USDe, a synthetic dollar collateralized by delta-neutral basis trades (long spot ETH/BTC/SOL hedged with short perpetual futures on CEXes). Yield from funding rates and staked-ETH rewards is paid to sUSDe holders via an ERC4626 vault with a 1-7 day dynamic unstake cooldown (90-day contract cap). Direct mint/redeem at par is restricted to KYC-whitelisted counterparties via the EthenaMinting contract; retail users acquire and exit USDe through secondary markets (Curve, Uniswap) or via the sUSDe vault.

headline: USDe redemption is operator-mediated and can be disabled with no on-chain expiry

findings.text: The deployed exit surfaces checked were EthenaMintingV2.redeem(Order,Signature) for USDe-to-collateral redemption, and StakedUSDeV2.withdraw, redeem, cooldownAssets, cooldownShares, and unstake for sUSDe-to-USDe exit. USDe burn/burnFrom destroys USDe but does not return backing, so it was not treated as an exit.

EthenaMintingV2.redeem is external but restricted by onlyRole(REDEEMER_ROLE), nonReentrant, per-asset and global max-redeem-per-block checks, and order verification. StakedUSDeV2.withdraw and redeem require ensureCooldownOff, cooldownAssets and cooldownShares require ensureCooldownOn, and unstake checks cooldownEnd or cooldownDuration==0 without a role modifier.

The pause-equivalent control for USDe redemption is cap-based: DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE can set global/per-asset redeem caps, and GATEKEEPER_ROLE can set global mint and redeem caps to zero via disableMintRedeem, with no auto-expiry in the deployed source. Constructor evidence identifies 0x3B0AAf6e6fCd4a7cEEf8c92C32DFeA9E64dC1862 as the admin, and Etherscan identifies that address as an Ethena Safe multisig; Ethena's repository describes the admin multisig as 7-of-10.

The emergency path is GATEKEEPER_ROLE.disableMintRedeem, which can only disable mint/redeem and remove hot roles; the governance/admin path is DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE, which can set redeem caps, manage roles, and later re-enable. The deployed source does not impose a time cap on either the zero-cap state or role removal.

sUSDe exits are queued when cooldownDuration is nonzero: cooldownAssets/cooldownShares burn shares into the USDeSilo and set cooldownEnd to block.timestamp plus cooldownDuration, while setCooldownDuration is admin-only and capped by MAX_COOLDOWN_DURATION=90 days. I found no sUSDe daily withdrawal cap in the checked staking source, but USDe collateral redemption has per-block caps that admin/gatekeeper can zero.

I found no permissionless forced-exit or escape-hatch that lets a USDe holder redeem backing if the Ethena redeemer role, backend order flow, whitelist, or redeem caps are unavailable. The repository describes secondary-market sale as the alternative for large or unavailable direct redemptions, not an on-chain forced exit.

The deployed contracts are verified on Etherscan and expose the relevant write functions directly, so sUSDe exit and authorized USDe redemption transactions can be submitted without Ethena's frontend; however USDe redemption itself still requires a REDEEMER_ROLE caller and a valid signed order flow.

steelman.red: USDe collateral redemption requires an authorized REDEEMER_ROLE operator and can be disabled indefinitely by zeroing redeem caps, so users cannot unilaterally exit to backing under adversarial admin/operator conditions.
steelman.orange: sUSDe holders have on-chain cooldown and claim functions, finalized unstake claims are not explicitly pause-gated in StakedUSDeV2, and the documented staking cooldown is capped at 90 days rather than unbounded.
steelman.green: A narrow view limited only to normal, unrestricted sUSDe-to-USDe unstaking would show direct Etherscan-callable functions and no separate frontend dependency.

rationale.verdict: Choosing red because the main USDe redemption path is not permissionless: EthenaMintingV2.redeem is only callable by REDEEMER_ROLE, requires signed order verification, and the admin/gatekeeper controls can set redeem capacity to zero with no source-level expiry, while no permissionless forced redemption path was found.

evidence.shows: Deployed Ethena Mint and Redeem V2 contract on Ethereum; constructor ties USDe to 0x4c9EDD5852cd905f086C759E8383e09bff1E68B3 and sets _admin to 0x3B0AAf6e6fCd4a7cEEf8c92C32DFeA9E64dC1862; source exposes redeem, cap setters, disableMintRedeem, role checks, and order verification.

Deployed StakedUSDeV2 contract on Ethereum; verified source lists withdraw, redeem, unstake, cooldownAssets, cooldownShares, setCooldownDuration, MAX_COOLDOWN_DURATION=90 days, and blacklist/restriction logic.
Deployed USDe ERC-20 token contract on Ethereum, used as the asset in StakedUSDeV2 and the token burned by EthenaMintingV2 redemption.
Etherscan labels the admin address used by the assessed contracts as Ethena multisig wallets and identifies it as a Safe smart account.

Ethena repository README describes USDe mint/redeem as Ethena-backend-submitted EIP-712 orders, the gatekeeper disable mechanism, sUSDe cooldown up to 90 days, restriction roles, and a 7-of-10 Gnosis Safe ownership model.

Ethena role-verification repository identifies the tracked EthenaMinting and StakedUSDeV2 contract addresses and the roles it scans, including DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE, REDEEMER_ROLE, GATEKEEPER_ROLE, BLACKLIST_MANAGER_ROLE, and restriction roles.
Ethena audits index lists Zellic, Quantstamp, Spearbit, Pashov, Code4rena, and Chaos Labs reports with report links and dates.

admin_addresses.role: DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE / owner multisig for EthenaMintingV2 and StakedUSDeV2

protocol_metadata.about: Ethena issues USDe, a synthetic dollar minted against crypto collateral and maintained through delta-hedged backing positions. Qualified users mint and redeem USDe through signed RFQ-style orders submitted on-chain by Ethena operators, while holders can stake USDe into sUSDe to receive a yield-bearing ERC-4626-style vault token. sUSDe exits burn shares into an escrow silo and require a cooldown before claiming USDe.

headline: Redemption requires whitelisting and off-chain protocol signatures

findings.text: The primary exit function for converting USDe to collateral is redeem() in the EthenaMinting contract. For sUSDe holders, the exit path involves cooldownAssets() or cooldownShares() followed by unstake() after a timer expires.

The redeem() function in EthenaMinting is protected by the onlyRole(REDEEMER_ROLE) modifier and the whenNotPaused guard. The cooldownAssets() function in StakedUSDeV2 is protected by the whenNotPaused guard but does not require a whitelist role for sUSDe holders to return to USDe.

The EthenaMinting contract can be paused indefinitely by the DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE or PAUSE_ROLE, both held by the Ethena admin multisig (0x2b0730533298960823393088788f93c75d059503). There is no on-chain mechanism to force an unpause or a maximum pause duration.
The sUSDe contract implements a cooldown period (currently 7 days) via the cooldownDuration variable. This duration can be modified by the admin up to a MAX_COOLDOWN_DURATION of 90 days.

The redeem() function in EthenaMinting requires an EIP-712 signature from the protocol's backend (MINTER_ROLE). This creates a hard dependency on Ethena's off-chain infrastructure; users cannot redeem USDe for collateral directly on-chain if the backend is unavailable or refuses to sign.

steelman.red: The protocol-level exit from USDe to collateral is restricted to whitelisted actors and requires a mandatory off-chain signature from the protocol's backend, preventing permissionless redemption.
steelman.orange: While USDe redemption is gated, the sUSDe staking contract provides a documented 7-day cooldown for users to reclaim USDe, and the token's primary exit path is through highly liquid external secondary markets.
steelman.green: The gated redemption model is a necessary security and stability feature for a delta-neutral synthetic dollar, and the protocol has functioned as intended through multiple high-volatility events.

rationale.verdict: Choosing red because the primary mechanism for exiting the protocol (redeeming USDe for collateral) is not permissionless; it requires the caller to hold the REDEEMER_ROLE (whitelisting) and provide an EIP-712 signature generated by Ethena's off-chain minting service, which directly violates the requirement for an on-chain, non-custodial exit path.

evidence.shows: EthenaMinting contract showing redeem() function with onlyRole(REDEEMER_ROLE) and signature requirements.
StakedUSDeV2 contract showing cooldown mechanism and MAX_COOLDOWN_DURATION of 90 days.
Official documentation confirming that users must receive a signed order from Ethena's minting service to redeem USDe.
Source code for EthenaMinting confirming the signature and role-based access control for redemptions.

unknowns: E4: No distinction between emergency and governance pause paths was found in the contract code; both appear to use the same standard Pausable logic.

protocol_metadata.about: Ethena is a synthetic dollar protocol providing a crypto-native solution for money not reliant on traditional banking system infrastructure. It uses delta-neutral hedging of staked Ethereum collateral across centralized and decentralized exchanges to maintain the USDe peg and generate yield for sUSDe holders.
